description: Feel a favourable response or easy affinity towards a person, activity, thing, or quality. Liking may guide a preference, but 'kanu' (choose) names the selection itself and can follow other reasons. 'lothea' (love) carries deeper affection or care; similarity uses 'phea' (like, as) and is unrelated to this verb.
articulatory_notes: Close the back of the tongue against the soft palate for 'k' and release into open central 'a'. Move forward into stressed middle-height front unrounded 'e' as a separate syllable across the 'a.e' hiatus. Touch the tongue tip to the alveolar ridge for 'l' and finish with high-front unrounded 'i'.
sound_symbolism: The velar release opens across a full hiatus, then the lateral carries the word into bright final 'i'. That light, rising contour suits an ordinary positive response without making it as weighty as love or commitment.
usage_notes: Whatever is liked comes before 'kaeli'. Comparative 'mo' can state which of two alternatives is liked more. Resemblance belongs to preposition 'phea', never to this verb.

semantic_domains.aesthetic: a favourable response to a sensory or artistic quality
semantic_domains.emotion: ordinary liking or affinity towards a person, activity, thing, or quality
